text:
Simone Biles has defended her decision to bow forOlympicchampion Rebeca Andrade during a medal ceremony after oneNFLstar branded the act of sportsmanship ‘disgusting’.

Biles and fellow American gymnast Jordan Chiles bowed down to Olympic gold medallist Andrade after the Brazilian won the floor final inParis.

After claiming gold in three events in the French capital, Biles was going for a fourth victory buthad to settle for silver after making a couple of uncharacteristic mistakes during her routine.

Biles was initially disappointed by the outcome but soon celebrated team-mate Chiles’ bronze and paid tribute to Brazilian gymnast Andrade, producing one of the most iconic images of the Games.

The vast majority of fans praised Biles and Chiles for their sportsmanship but Baltimore Ravens cornerback Marlon Humphrey bizarrely called it ‘disgusting’.

‘This is literally disgusting,’ the 28-year-old posted on X, before adding: ‘I’m lowkey getting cooked right now. I think I’m getting cussed out in Brazilian on Instagram lol.’

Speaking onNBC’s TODAYshow, Biles defended the gesture and said it was important for the Americans to give Andrade her ‘flowers’.

This is everything.pic.twitter.com/FrXz7wWtQg

‘I think it’s all about sportsmanship, and we don’t care whether we win or lose,’ the 11-time Olympic medal winner said.

‘We’re always going to keep a good face and support our competitors because they’ve worked just as hard as we have for that moment.

‘So you have to give them their flowers. And that’s exactly what me and Jordan were doing, and we were so happy for her.

‘She deserved it. She had the best floor routine of the day and in the Olympics. So it’s like, yeah, she deserved it.’

In a separate interview, Biles described Andrade as a ‘queen’ and said it was ‘super-exciting’ to be a part of the first all-black podium in men or women’s gymnastics at the Olympics.

‘Rebeca’s so amazing, she’s a queen,’ said Biles. ‘She’s such an excitement to watch and then all the fans in the crowd were always cheering for her, so it was just the right thing to do.

‘It was an all-black podium so that was super-exciting for us, but then Jordan was like “should we bow to her?” and I was like “absolutely”.’

Andrade, meanwhile, appreciated the gesture from the American duo, saying: ‘It was very cute of them.

‘They are the world’s best athletes and what they did means a lot to me. We’re always rooting for each other, the final is very difficult for everyone.’

Paris 2024 is expected to be Biles’ final Olympic Games but she has not ruled out competing in 2028 when the show heads to Los Angeles.

‘You know, you never say never,’ she said. ‘The next Olympics is on home turf, so you just never know. I’m just gonna relax and see where life takes me.’

title: Simone Biles responds after her Olympic gesture is branded 'disgusting' | Metro News
description: Simone Biles has defended her decision to bow for Olympic champion Rebeca Andrade during a medal ceremony after it was called 'disgusting'.
